description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: test, build, and deploy. Use docker:stable as the image for the build, dependency_scanning, and sast jobs (each with a docker:stable-dind service and DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2, DOCKER_HOST=tcp://docker:2375), and python:3.6 for the test job (tagged docker). In the build stage add a build job that runs: docker login $CI_REGISTRY -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD; docker build --build-arg VERSION=$CI_COMMIT_SHA . -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_PIPELINE_IID; docker tag $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_PIPELINE_IID $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest; docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest; docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_PIPELINE_IID. In the test stage add a test job that runs pip install -e . and python test.py with FLASK_TESTING='True', and also add dependency_scanning and sast jobs (both allow_failure) that export SP_VERSION from $CI_SERVER_VERSION, then execute docker run with the appropriate security‑scanner image (registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/dependency-scanning:$SP_VERSION and .../sast:$SP_VERSION) mounting the repository and /var/run/docker.sock, passing environment variables DEP_SCAN_DISABLE_REMOTE_CHECKS or SAST_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL, and produce artifact reports gl-dependency-scanning-report.json and gl-sast-report respectively. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that only runs on the master branch, downloads kubectl, makes it executable, writes $KUBE_CONFIG (base64‑decoded) to $KUBECONFIG, configures credentials with $KUBE_TOKEN, then updates the Kubernetes deployment "builds" in namespace builds to use the image $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_PIPELINE_IID, waits for rollout status with a 60s timeout and rolls back on failure.
